title: Sanitize CUDA wheel stacks for torch-npu environments
symptom: A CUDA-oriented requirements stack pins nvidia-* cu12 wheels, triton, flash-attn, torch/torchaudio/torchvision CUDA-linked variants, and numpy 2.x. On Ascend NPU this can install incompatible CUDA binaries or fail imports, including torchaudio attempting to load libtorch_cuda.so.
root_cause: The original dependency manifest targets a CUDA PyTorch runtime rather than an Ascend torch-npu runtime. CUDA wheel families such as nvidia-*cu12, CUDA triton, flash-attn, and CUDA-linked torchaudio are not valid runtime dependencies for the NPU environment, while the observed CANN 8.x torch-npu stack required the host-compatible torch CPU wheel family, matching torch-npu, PyYAML, and numpy 1.26.x.
fix_steps: 1. Before creating the virtual environment, scan the project dependency files for CUDA-only package families: nvidia-*cu12, triton, flash-attn, and unqualified torch, torchaudio, or torchvision pins.
2. Do not install nvidia-*cu12 packages, CUDA triton, or flash-attn into the torch-npu environment.

3. Create the project virtual environment without modifying the global Python environment; in this run, the working approach used python3 -m venv --system-site-packages .venv to inherit the host NPU stack.
4. Install the NPU-compatible core stack: torch==2.5.1+cpu, torch-npu==2.5.1, torchvision==0.20.1+cpu, torchaudio==2.5.1+cpu, numpy==1.26.4, and PyYAML==6.0.2.

5. Install the non-CUDA application dependencies from the project requirements, preserving needed packages such as transformers==4.46.1, accelerate==1.3.0, gradio==5.13.1, librosa==0.10.2.post1, and soundfile==0.13.1.

6. If torchaudio was installed from a mirror and importing it fails with a CUDA-linked libtorch_cuda.so error, force reinstall the CPU wheel with --no-deps from the PyTorch CPU wheel index: torchaudio==2.5.1+cpu.
7. Probe the environment before Phase 5 validation by importing torch, torch_npu, torchvision, and torchaudio, then checking that torch.npu is available and selects the intended Ascend device.
8. Run the migration validation entry only after dependency import probes pass; in the source run, final validation exercised t2t, t2s, s2t, and s2s on npu:0 with exit_code=0.

code_changes.before:
numpy==2.1.3
nvidia-cuda-runtime-cu12==12.4.127
nvidia-nccl-cu12==2.21.5
torch==2.5.1
torchaudio==2.5.1
torchvision==0.20.1
triton==3.1.0
code_changes.after:
torch==2.5.1+cpu
torch-npu==2.5.1
torchvision==0.20.1+cpu
torchaudio==2.5.1+cpu
numpy==1.26.4
PyYAML==6.0.2

python3 -m venv --system-site-packages .venv
.venv/bin/python -m pip install non-CUDA project dependencies
.venv/bin/python -m pip install --force-reinstall --no-deps torchaudio==2.5.1+cpu --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu

antipatterns: Do NOT install nvidia-*cu12 wheels in a torch-npu runtime environment.
Do NOT install CUDA triton or flash-attn for Ascend NPU validation.
Do NOT leave torchaudio as a CUDA-linked wheel if import errors reference libtorch_cuda.so.
Do NOT silently fall back to CPU for model, codec, tensor, or audio-token computation when the migration target requires NPU execution.
Do NOT use numpy>=2.0 with this observed CANN 8.x torch-npu stack if it causes ACL/CANN initialization failures.

meta.refiner_reasoning: The artifacts show the original requirements contained CUDA-specific packages and numpy 2.1.3, the constraint summary required removing CUDA-only dependency resolution, the created venv used torch CPU wheels plus torch-npu and numpy 1.26.4, and the tools report documents replacing CUDA-linked torchaudio with torchaudio==2.5.1+cpu after a libtorch_cuda.so failure. Final validation passed all four modes on npu:0.

signals: migration target is Ascend NPU with torch-npu
trigger_fingerprint.recommended_action: sanitize dependency manifest before venv creation and validate torch, torch_npu, torchaudio, torchvision, and torch.npu availability

classifier.reasoning: The candidate describes a reusable, actionable procedure for sanitizing CUDA-oriented PyTorch dependency stacks during torch-npu migration: remove CUDA wheel families, replace PyTorch ecosystem packages with CPU host wheels plus matching torch-npu, constrain numpy/PyYAML for CANN compatibility, and verify imports/NPU availability before validation. This is more than a mechanical one-line transformation because it requires environment-aware package selection and validation sequencing.

signals: migration target is Ascend NPU with torch-npu
trigger_fingerprint.recommended_action: sanitize dependency manifest before venv creation and validate torch, torch_npu, torchaudio, torchvision, and torch.npu availability

problem_description: The source requirements pinned CUDA-oriented packages including nvidia-* cu12 wheels, triton, torch/torchaudio CUDA-linked variants, and numpy 2.x. On Ascend NPU this can install incompatible CUDA binaries or trigger import/runtime failures such as CUDA-linked torchaudio requiring libtorch_cuda.so.
rough_fix_approach: Filter requirements before venv creation: remove nvidia-* CUDA wheels, CUDA triton, and flash-attn; install host-compatible torch CPU wheels plus matching torch-npu; pin torchvision/torchaudio CPU variants, PyYAML, and numpy<2 for CANN 8.x compatibility; verify imports and torch.npu availability before validation.
name: sanitize-cuda-wheel-stack-for-torch-npu
